what_it_does: Defines a shared fourth "Convergence Round" that fires after three build lanes (cognition, coherence, guided-review) each report lane-complete and self-verified. It specifies the trigger, four focuses (converge+merge, system-wide check, end-to-end usage verification, polish), an ownership proposal, and co-authored refinements and concrete scenarios from the coherence and cognition sessions.
observations: Proposes a shared round that sits on top of per-lane gates without replacing them.
Trigger is three "lane-complete" markers in a shared folder, one from each lane.
Four focuses: converge+merge, system-wide check, end-to-end usage verification (the operator spine), and polish.

Coherence adds three push-backs: shared-main already means no big-bang git merge; gates run continuously while the full round fires at lane-complete; system-wide check has both structural gates and semantic by-use parts.

Cognition agrees and contributes five concrete engine-seam by-use scenarios (C fires for walkthrough, run_items resolves role inputs by address, run_reduce live clustering, mode-dial 13 axes, gated launch/select capability).
Ownership proposal: cognition owns engine seams, coherence owns system-wide gates + continuous cadence, guided-review owns operator-path-by-use + orchestration + reconcile, with joint sign-off.
Repeatedly invokes project laws: verify-by-use, no-green-paint, surface-don't-defer.
No code symbols; the file is entirely prose, planning, and coordination.
standouts: "Verify the seam, not the cell" generalized from two-stream convergence to three parallel lanes.
Coherence's push-back redefining the "merge" focus as continuous-integration verification over an already-shared main, not a destructive three-way git merge.
Two-cadence model: cheap structural gates run on every shared commit; expensive full convergence round fires only at lane-complete.
Cognition's by-use scenarios make abstract engine seams testable through real operator paths.
Joint sign-off requiring all three lenses plus an operator path proven by use, not green-painted.
conventions: Follows an RFC-style co-author document with dated sections and session-signed paragraphs.
Uses markdown blockquotes, headers, bullet lists, and bold emphasis for claims.
Breaks the conventional "final merge" metaphor by treating integration as verification over an already-interleaved shared main.
Relies heavily on project-specific jargon: lanes, C, run_items, run_reduce, mode_registry, claims board, company suites, no-green-paint.
concerns: Several items remain explicitly OPEN: trigger mechanism, exact end-to-end scenarios, polish bar specifics.
Ownership is labeled a "proposal — settle here" and still contains unresolved details.
Dense jargon and acronyms may make the document inaccessible outside the three sessions.
Some scenarios depend on future capabilities that are not yet landed (e.g., "if the corpus-chain lands by then", "after C 4/4").
notes: A three-lane integration protocol that uses continuous gate-running as the safety net and reserves a final shared round for seam verification, operator-by-use proof, and joint sign-off.
questions: What is the exact format and location of a "lane-complete" marker?
Who or what triggers the full round once the three markers exist — a human, a bot, or a shared cron?
What are the concrete end-to-end scenarios from the guided-review angle?
What is the polish bar and who has final say on whether it is met?
What happens if the full convergence round fails — revert, fix-forward, or block further shared commits?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to plan a shared convergence round; the file does exactly that, while also collecting co-authored push-backs and scenarios. It remains a draft with open items.
apparent_themes: Verify the seam, not the cell.
No green-painting; verify by real use.
Shared main and continuous integration over big-bang merging.
Honesty-as-gate: the substrate holds all builders to one standard.
Project laws bind the build (surface-don't-defer, verify-by-use).
intention_signals: It is trying to institutionalize a three-way "verify the whole" checkpoint so parallel work on cognition, coherence, and guided-review does not leave seams untested, and to make the convergence round the first real consumer of the coherence substrate as a shared honesty instrument.
novelty: Generalizes a previously proven "remediation-build" seam-verification pattern to three parallel forks and explicitly splits structural gate checks from semantic by-use checks.
contribution: A concrete, jointly authored integration protocol with a two-cadence verification model, explicit seam scenarios for each session, and a shared sign-off discipline that preserves per-lane autonomy while making whole-system behavior provable.
summary_for_embedding: A coordination document defining the Convergence Round, a shared final verification pass over three parallel build lanes that checks seams, runs system-wide gates, proves end-to-end operator paths by use, and requires joint sign-off.
intention_for_embedding: To plan up front how cognition, coherence, and guided-review will integrate and prove the whole system works once all three lanes are self-verified.
